betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Guidance Computer§f is the §6flight controller§f for every AR rocket. It is a §6single block§f linked to the §6Rocket Assembling Machine§f (10022) via a §6Linker§f, and it reads the §6Planet ID Chip§f (advancedrocketry:planetidchip) inserted into the assembler to set the §6destination dimension§f. Without a guidance computer the assembler will not spawn a rocket entity.

§6Production Path (guidancecomputer.json)§f:
- Type: §6minecraft:crafting_shaped§f
- Pattern (3×3):
  ```
  c t c
  r b r
  c r c
  ```
- Key: `c`=§6advancedrocketry:ic:1§f (Tracking Circuit), `t`=§6plateTitanium§f, `r`=§6dustRedstone§f, `b`=§6libvulpes:structuremachine§f (Machine Structure)
- Output: 1x §6Guidance Computer§f (advancedrocketry:guidancecomputer)
- The CT script `advancedRocketryTweaks.zs` does not modify this recipe — the AR JSON is the canonical one

The guidance computer stores the §6destination ID§f, the §6return trip settings§f, and a §6max safe altitude§f for the rocket. It also drives the §6Planet Holo Selector§f (advancedrocketry:planetholoselector) when linked to a §6Holographic Planet Selector§f — the questline item that gives the player a 3D preview of the destination world.

The guidance computer is a prerequisite for the §6Planet Holo Selector§f (chain) and the §6Space Station Assembler§f (chain). It is the single most important infrastructure tile for any §6planetary mission§f because the §6Rocket Assembling Machine§f rejects any rocket without a linked guidance computer.

§eTip§f: Right-click the guidance computer with a §6Planet ID Chip§f to set the destination. The chip must have been §6researched§f by the §6Astrobody Data Processor§f (10020) — an unresearched chip will not bind and the GUI will report "Invalid destination".
